reasoning: The baseline fertility rate from 2023 is 0.72. Preliminary reports from Statistics Korea confirmed that the fertility rate rose to 0.75 in 2024 due to a surge in marriages. This momentum continued robustly into 2025, with the Q1 2025 fertility rate reaching 0.82. Recent data reported in late 2025 shows that births have increased for at least 15 consecutive months, and September 2025 birth registrations were 11.5% higher than the previous year, with the total fertility rate reportedly hitting 0.85 in September. Given that 2024 was already higher than 2023 (0.75 vs 0.72), and 2025 has seen even stronger growth across all available quarters, it is mathematically near-certain that the total fertility rate for 2025 will be higher than the 0.72 mark. 0.95 is selected as the upper bound of highly confident probabilities according to guidelines.
